title: Scope & Blocks 📦
description: Understand where your variables live and when they die.

slides.title: The Circle of Life ♻️
slides.content:
Variables do not live forever. A variable's lifetime is tied strictly to its **Scope**.

A scope is defined by a block of code surrounded by curly braces `{ ... }`.

Once a scope inevitably ends at the closing brace `}`, the variables inside it are immediately destroyed.

slides.title: Visualizing Scope 📦
slides.content: Notice how the inner scope variables do not leak outside.

quizData.question: What happens to a variable when its scope (block) ends?

options.text: It is saved to the hard drive
It is destroyed and its memory is instantly freed

quizData.explanation: Rust automatically frees memory when variables drop out of scope at the closing curly brace.

slides.title: Shadowing inside Scopes 🥷
slides.content:
Shadowing works incredibly well with scopes.

If you shadow an outer variable *inside* a nested block `{ }`, the shadow disappears once the block ends, revealing the original outer variable again!

slides.title: Peekaboo! 👻
slides.content: An example of shadowing inside blocks.

quizData.question: If you shadow a variable entirely within a block `{ }`, what happens after the block ends?

options.text: The outer variable's original value is revealed again
The shadowed value overwrites the global state

quizData.explanation: The inner shadowed variable is destroyed at the end of the block, leaving the outer unmodified variable exactly as it was.
